step_by_step_thinking: I agree completely. To further support this point, it’s worth noting that even though radiographs might miss some aspects of an acute alveolar abscess initially, clinicians usually rely on additional diagnostic tools such as intraoral exams, palpation, and sometimes contrast-enhanced imaging methods if needed. However, these supplementary tests confirm what initial radiographs alone couldn't capture about the nature and extent of the condition. My final answer remains C: Acute alveolar abscess.
